L1.area: HTTP request processing and S3 API middleware layer

L2.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the S3-compatible gateway. When chunked uploads arrive without a Content-Length HTTP header—for example when requests pass through a reverse proxy that strips or does not forward certain transfer-related headers—the underlying HTTP framework assumes there is no request body and sets the body reader to nil. Subsequent processing that attempts to read the request body triggers a panic, causing a denial of service.

L3.area: S3 API middleware responsible for preparing and wrapping the HTTP request body reader for authentication and checksum processing
L3.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the S3-compatible gateway's middleware that prepares request body readers. When AWS chunked uploads are routed through a reverse proxy that strips the Transfer-Encoding header, the HTTP framework does not create a body reader because it sees no Content-Length. The middleware code that wraps or assigns the body reader does not check for a nil value before passing it to downstream handlers for authentication and checksum verification, causing a panic and denial of service.

ground_truth.reason: When chunked uploads arrive without Transfer-Encoding or Content-Length headers (e.g. through a reverse proxy), the HTTP framework sets the request body reader to nil, causing a panic when middleware attempts to read from it.
